summarization.a: Predicted minimum-energy structure of a MeCpPtMe₃-derived species chemisorbed on pristine graphene, showing non-covalent interactions with Pt remaining above the surface. Chemisorption energy calculated at the PBE-D3 level.
summarization.b: Predicted minimum-energy structure of a MeCpPtMe₃-derived species chemisorbed on pristine graphene, showing covalent bonding of Pt to two neighboring carbon atoms, highlighting stronger species-dependent binding behavior. Chemisorption energy calculated at the PBE-D3 level.
summarization.c: Predicted minimum-energy structure of another MeCpPtMe₃-derived species on pristine graphene, exhibiting non-covalent interactions with Pt located above the surface. Chemisorption energy calculated at the PBE-D3 level.
summarization.d: Predicted minimum-energy structure of Pt(acac)₂ chemisorbed on pristine graphene, showing non-covalent interactions with Pt remaining above the graphene surface. Chemisorption energy calculated at the PBE-D3 level.

a.question: Does the removal of ligands from MeCpPtMe₃ influence the binding of Pt on graphene?
a.answer: Yes. Removing ligands exposes d-orbital electrons on Pt, enabling covalent bonding to graphene in certain species, as seen in MeCpPtMe.

b.question: Which MeCpPtMe₃-derived species exhibits covalent bonding to graphene, and how does it compare to other species?
b.answer: MeCpPtMe (panel b) exhibits covalent bonding to two neighboring carbon atoms, whereas MeCpPtMe₂ (panel a) and MeCpPtCH₃CH₂ (panel c) remain non-covalently bound at larger distances (~3.9–4.1 Å).

d.question: Explain how understanding the binding behavior of different Pt species on graphene can guide the design of ALD processes.
d.answer: Understanding the binding behavior of Pt species on graphene informs which precursors can effectively deposit Pt. Species that covalently bond, such as MeCpPtMe, are more likely to act as nucleation sites for controlled Pt deposition, whereas non-covalently bound species remain mobile and may not contribute to film growth. Insights into ligand effects, orbital availability, and steric hindrance help optimize precursor selection and ALD conditions for uniform and area-selective Pt deposition on graphene surfaces.

c.question: List the key factors that determine the binding behavior of Pt species on pristine graphene.
c.answer: Availability of d-orbital electrons on Pt, Removal of ligands from the Pt precursor, Saturation of graphene valence orbitals, Steric effects of methyl or larger substituents, Nature of the Pt precursor (MeCpPtMe₃ vs Pt(acac)₂)
